verdict_reasoning: STIM1 is an ER-resident transmembrane protein with a single-pass TM domain that anchors it to the ER membrane. Upon ER Ca2+ depletion, STIM1 undergoes a conformational change, oligomerizes, and translocates to ER-PM junctional contacts, where its cytoplasmic domains directly interact with and activate the PM-resident Orai1 Ca2+ channel. The N-terminal EF-hand and SAM domain (luminal/ER-facing) sense Ca2+ depletion; the C-terminal coiled-coil domains (cytoplasmic-facing) engage Orai1 at the PM. STIM1 reaches the outer face of the plasma membrane as a stable component of the ER-PM junction during store-operated calcium entry (SOCE). The protein is tethered to the PM via direct protein-protein interaction with Orai1 and possibly other junction scaffolds; it is not secreted, not merely transiently recruited, but rather a bona-fide participant in a membrane-anchored signaling complex that physically bridges the ER and PM.
